fields.icd_description: Unspecified dislocation of right shoulder joint
fields.icd_conflict_detail: The referral form (Document 1) lists ICD-10 code S40.011A (Contusion of right shoulder), while both the clinical notes (Document 2) and prescription (Document 3) consistently list S43.004A (Unspecified dislocation of right shoulder joint). Per resolution protocol, clinical notes and prescription take authority over the referral form; S43.004A was selected as the correct code. Additionally, the medical justification language referencing 'safe ambulation' and 'falls prevention' appears inconsistent with a shoulder immobilizer regardless of diagnosis, and may warrant clinical review.

icd_conflicts.label: Primary Diagnosis
icd_conflicts.reasoning: Clinical notes and prescription both consistently document S43.004A (dislocation) versus the referral form's S40.011A (contusion), so S43.004A was selected as the authoritative code per resolution protocol, though a clinical review is advisable given the boilerplate-sounding medical justification language.

fields.priority_reason: Shoulder dislocation is a moderate orthopedic injury requiring an immobilizer brace; while there is an ICD conflict, it was resolved with sufficient confidence (85) and does not require escalation, placing this case in the medium priority tier.
fields.notes: 1) ICD-10 conflict detected: referral form lists S40.011A (contusion) while clinical notes and prescription list S43.004A (dislocation) — resolved in favor of S43.004A. 2) The medical justification text ('support safe ambulation and prevent falls in home environment') is atypical for a shoulder immobilizer and appears to be boilerplate language more suitable for a mobility aid; this warrants clinical review to confirm appropriateness. 3) The referral form describes the diagnosis as 'Contusion of right shoulder' while clinical notes describe active treatment including injection therapy and physical therapy for a dislocation — the treating physician should confirm the correct clinical picture. 4) Prescription is valid through 09/25/2026 (90 days from 06/28/2026).

kg_validation.source: CMS ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines for Coding and Reporting, FY2026

icd_conflicts.label: Primary Diagnosis
icd_conflicts.reasoning: Clinical notes and prescription both consistently document S43.004A (dislocation) versus the referral form's S40.011A (contusion), so S43.004A was selected as the authoritative code per resolution protocol, though a clinical review is advisable given the boilerplate-sounding medical justification language.
